name: Pricing Engine — priceOrder
evidence: src/pricing/engine.ts exports priceOrder with SPEC §2 types and loader-based data access
No TODO/FIXME markers remain under src/ after OrderPage wiring was completed
VS Code diagnostics report 0 errors in src/pricing/engine.ts
Known traps from pricingV1.ts are avoided in the current implementation (best-for-customer, half-up rounding, inclusive validTo, repeating BOGO)
expectedBehavior: Export priceOrder(input: PriceOrderInput): PricedOrder. Applies active, eligible promotions (percent_off, bogo, threshold) using best-for-customer selection. Uses half-up rounding to 2dp on every money field. Throws on unknown productId/accountId or invalid qty.
currentGap: Implemented. Local build/test execution remains environment-limited because the workspace snapshot is missing the installed Vite payload used by the normal scripts.
suggestedApproach: Retain the current loader-based implementation and rerun build/test in a complete install before submission.
verificationStrategy: VS Code diagnostics: pass. oxlint direct run: pass. Full build/test should be re-run once vite/client and vite package files are present locally.
knownTraps: src/legacy/pricingV1.ts uses cumulative stacking — SPEC says best-for-customer, one promo per line
src/legacy/pricingV1.ts uses banker's rounding — SPEC says half-up
src/legacy/pricingV1.ts treats validTo as exclusive — SPEC says inclusive
src/legacy/pricingV1.ts caps BOGO at one group — SPEC says repeating groups
docs/NOTES.md 2025-10-21 says threshold qualifies on gross — SPEC says on line nets (post-discount)
docs/NOTES.md 2025-09-12 says validTo exclusive (ERP convention) — SPEC says inclusive

name: Shelf Audit — auditAccounts
evidence: src/audit/shelfAudit.ts exports auditAccounts with SPEC §10 shape
VS Code diagnostics report 0 errors in src/audit/shelfAudit.ts
Implementation uses getAccounts/getVisits from the shared typed loader surface
expectedBehavior: Export auditAccounts(asOf: string): AccountAudit[]. One entry per account, sorted by accountId asc. Each entry has weightedScore (weighted average of up to 3 most recent visits), trend (up/down/flat/null), daysSinceVisit, overdue (>14 days or no visits), and status (unvisited/critical/watch/healthy). Throws on invalid asOf format.
currentGap: Implemented. Full build/test execution remains blocked by the incomplete local Vite install snapshot, not by editor diagnostics on this module.
suggestedApproach: Keep the current weighted-score implementation and re-run build/test in a complete install before submission.
verificationStrategy: VS Code diagnostics: pass. oxlint direct run: pass. Full build/test pending a complete local vite package.
knownTraps: docs/NOTES.md suggests renaming shelfScore to merchandisingIndex — SPEC uses shelfScore, ignore the rename
daysSinceVisit=14 is NOT overdue; only strictly >14 is overdue

name: Route Settlement — settleRoute
evidence: src/settlement/settle.ts exports settleRoute and imports priceOrder from the pricing engine
VS Code diagnostics report 0 errors in src/settlement/settle.ts
Implementation aggregates money totals, category nets, promo usage, commission, and stop lists per SPEC §11
expectedBehavior: Export settleRoute(input: SettleRouteInput): RouteSettlement. Validates routeId and that each order's accountId is on the route. Prices each order via priceOrder. Aggregates grossTotal, lineDiscountTotal, orderDiscountTotal, discountTotal, netTotal. Builds perCategory (line nets by category, sorted asc). Builds promoUsage (counts, sorted asc). Computes marginal commission (2%/5%/8%). Determines stopsVisited and stopsMissed in route order.
currentGap: Implemented. Remaining verification risk is environmental because the local install is missing the Vite package required by the standard build/test commands.
suggestedApproach: Retain the current import-through-priceOrder design and rerun build/test in a complete install before submission.
verificationStrategy: VS Code diagnostics: pass. oxlint direct run: pass. Full build/test pending a complete local vite package.
knownTraps: Commission is MARGINAL (bracket-based), not a flat rate — a single-rate implementation is wrong
perCategory must exclude categories with zero net (absent, not 0)
stopsVisited deduplication: if an accountId appears twice in route stops, list it once at first position
Do NOT re-implement pricing — import priceOrder from ../pricing/engine
